title: Round Down
excerpt: Round Down is the default interpretation rule for fractional results. When a calculation produces a fraction and no other rule overrides it, the result resolves to the next lower whole number.
markdown:
# Round Down

Round Down is the default numeric interpretation rule when a rules calculation produces a fraction. Unless another rule explicitly says otherwise, the result resolves to the next lower whole number.

## Rules

- If a formula divides, halves, or otherwise produces a fractional value, the result rounds down by default
- Round Down applies only when the rule leaves a fractional remainder; whole-number results are unchanged
- A more specific rule can replace this convention with a different rounding method or with exact non-rounded handling
- Rounding is part of result interpretation after the calculation is produced, not a separate modifier to the calculation itself

## Use In Aralia

Round Down keeps numeric resolution stable when effects produce partial values. It prevents ambiguous fractions from lingering in damage, healing, scaling, or derived calculations unless a more specific rule preserves a different result.
